name: Date Picker
summary: The first named shallow preset over the abstract `temporal` core: a single calendar-date picker. It pins the Temporal Intent to presentation=media (a spatial calendar grid) and granularity=point (one date), bound to the native <input type="date"> anchor. It adds nothing the core lacks — it is the core with two dimensions pinned and one anchor chosen — so it inherits locale-aware parse/format, keyboard roving, and the native form-control baseline for free. Native <input type="date"> stays the zero-JS fallback; the calendar-grid surface is the media-presentation enhancement.
designDecisions: A preset is a dimension pin, not a new block of machinery (#713 option C). date-picker is the abstract `temporal` core with presentation=media + granularity=point pinned and the input[type=date] anchor bound; it re-implements none of the calendar/locale/keyboard machinery — that is the core's. This is the family rule (one core, members are pins) the same way the droplist family's members are trait selections over one substrate.

input[type=date] is the anchor and fallback. Following the slider precedent (native input is the baseline, custom surface only for the gap), the preset's media presentation renders a calendar grid for pointer/spatial selection while the native date input remains the keyboard-entry and no-JS fallback, carrying form participation, validity, and locale-aware value out of the box.

ariaApgKeyboard.usage: A single calendar-date preset: native `<input type="date">` or a grid calendar dialog with arrow-key day navigation.
